scenario: Helios Payments models a Camunda 8.8 settlement process that handles 8000 transactions per day. Inside one embedded subprocess the team needs to catch a specific business error from a service task while also providing a fallback handler for any other thrown error in the same scope.
question: Which catch event configuration is allowed inside the same embedded subprocess in Camunda 8.8 error events?

options.text: Two boundary events that both reference the same specific errorCode, attached to the same service task in the embedded subprocess for redundant routing of the same error
Two catch-all error event subprocesses inside the embedded subprocess, both with no errorRef so that any thrown error matches either one of the two catch-all subprocesses
One boundary event on the service task referencing the specific errorCode together with one catch-all error event subprocess inside the same embedded subprocess scope
One non-interrupting boundary error event on the service task together with one interrupting boundary error event on the same task for the same matching errorCode value

a.text: Incorrect. The Catching the error section states it is not possible to define multiple error catch events with the same errorCode in a single scope, so two boundary events with the same code are not allowed.

b.text: Incorrect. The page states it is not permitted to have multiple error catch-all events in a single scope, so two catch-all event subprocesses cannot coexist inside the same embedded subprocess.

c.text: Correct. The Catching the error section allows a specific errorCode catch event and an error catch-all event to coexist in the same scope; when both could match, the specific errorCode catch event is prioritized.

d.text: Incorrect. The page states that error boundary events and error event subprocesses must be interrupting, so a non-interrupting boundary error event is not a supported configuration in Camunda 8.8.

explanation: In a single scope you may pair one error catch event for a specific errorCode with one error catch-all event; the specific match wins when both could fire. All error boundary events and error event subprocesses must be interrupting.
